task: Write a Python function snake_to_camel(s) converting a snake_case identifier to camelCase, so 'foo_bar_baz' becomes 'fooBarBaz'. Leading, trailing, and repeated underscores are dropped: '__a__b_' becomes 'aB'. An empty string returns ''. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The code is concise, readable, and handles most edge cases correctly.
trace: I approached this task by splitting the given snake_case identifier into words using underscores as the delimiter, then lowercasing and capitalizing the first letter of each word. To handle the edge case where leading or trailing underscores are present, I checked if there are any words in the list before processing them. If there aren't any words (empty string), an empty string is returned. Lastly, to handle repeated underscores, I joined all the remaining words without any underscores. The solution provided is concise and readable while catering to the edge case specified.
